records.ety: [Akin to LG., D., & Dan. damp vapor, steam, fog, G. dampf, Icel. dampi, Sw. damb dust, and to MNG. dimpfen to smoke, imp. dampf.]

quotes: Night . . . with black air Accompanied, with damps and dreadful gloom.

defs: Dejection; depression; cloud of the mind.
quotes: Even now, while thus I stand blest in thy presence, A secret damp of grief comes o'er my soul.
It must have thrown a damp over your autumn excursion.

defs: A gaseous product, formed in coal mines, old wells, pints, etc.
Being in a state between dry and wet; moderately wet; moist; humid.
quotes: O'erspread with a damp sweat and holy fear.
All these and more came flocking, but with looks Downcast and damp.

records.ety: [OE. dampen to choke, suffocate. See Damp, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Damped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Damping.]

defs: To render damp; to moisten; to make humid, or moderately wet; to dampen; as, to damp cloth.
To put out, as fire; to depress or deject; to deaden; to cloud; to check or restrain, as action or vigor; to make dull; to weaken; to discourage.
quotes: Usury dulls and damps all industries, improvements, and new inventions, wherein money would be stirring if it were not for this slug.
How many a day has been damped and darkened by an angry word!
The failure of his enterprise damped the spirit of the soldiers.
